title: 'Keep doing my best, let God do the rest'
primary_topics: 'Keep doing my best, let God do the rest' personal credo
sub_topics: Rafa P. Yaptangco (grandson, Southridge School Alabang)
Ateneo de Manila University college admission essay
farmer and son parable ('Good news, bad news, who knows?')
Southridge School philosophy of forming men of integrity
Moving as One (MAO) cancer awareness charity

stances.claim: The opening message of the Ageless Passion musicale captured his life mantra: 'Good news, bad news, who knows? I will just keep on doing my best and let God do the rest.' — adapted from a folk parable of a farmer and his son experiencing alternating fortunes.
stances.rhetorical_move: transmission of personal credo via parable + intergenerational transmission to grandson

stances.claim: (via grandson Rafa) CJ's life shows that 'anyone willing to contribute a great deal of hard work for a better future can, indeed, have a better future' — a poor newsboy rising to the highest magistrate of the land through ageless passion.
stances.rhetorical_move: indirect self-presentation through grandson's voice + repetition of biographical-transformation formula

stances.claim: (via Rafa) Leadership requires setting up succession systems that allow organizations to continue when the founder graduates or leaves — a value Rafa applied to two Southridge clubs.
stances.rhetorical_move: transmission of CJ's institutional-continuation ethic via grandson's narration

signature_phrases: Keep doing my best, let God do the rest
Good news, bad news, who knows?
rags to riches, not in worldly wealth but in his multi-career fulfillment
from a frustrated chemical engineer to a reluctant lawyer to the highest magistrate of the land
register_markers: Last Sunday, instead of my usual commentary
extensive verbatim quotation as column body
decision_framework_signals: applies 'keep doing my best, let God do the rest' as personal action-providence framework
uses farmer-parable as folkloric epistemology of uncertainty
frames intergenerational transmission as the operative success metric for personal legacy
notable_anecdotes: His 75th birthday celebration musicale 'Ageless Passion' depicting his rise from poor newsboy to multi-career-fulfilled jurist
Grandson Rafa initially declining to play the younger CJ in the musicale due to fear of singing to a large audience, settling for a minor role
Rafa witnessing poverty for the first time through the choir of 20 boys performing to raise funds for their school
CJ at family dinners talking mostly about law and business — observed and quoted by his grandson
